hook.text: Amazon Web Services started as internal infrastructure. It accidentally became the backbone of the modern internet.

buildup.text: Amazon needed scalable computing for its own retail business — especially during holiday traffic spikes.

discovery.text: Andy Jassy realized other companies had the same scaling problem. What if Amazon rented its infrastructure to anyone?

twist.text: AWS launched in 2006 and was ignored by Microsoft, Google, and IBM. They didn't see a retailer as a tech infrastructure threat.

climax.text: AWS generates $90 billion per year, powering Netflix, NASA, and most startups. It's more profitable than Amazon's retail arm.

punchline.text: Solve your own problem. Then sell the solution to everyone else.

quiz.question: How did AWS become a transformative product?
options: Amazon solved its own infrastructure problem and rented that solution to other companies
AWS was always planned as Amazon's main business
Google created cloud computing and Amazon copied it
